HORIZONTAL_PUSH.description: Compound upper-body pressing movements benefit from staying 1–3 reps from failure.

VERTICAL_PUSH.description: Maintain technical quality while training close to failure.

HORIZONTAL_PULL.description: Focus on quality contractions while avoiding excessive fatigue.

VERTICAL_PULL.description: Pull close to failure while maintaining full range of motion.

SQUAT.description: Large lower-body compounds typically benefit from slightly more reserve for recovery.

HINGE.description: Hip hinge movements create high systemic fatigue; avoid frequent failure.

LUNGE.description: Train close to failure while maintaining balance and control.

ELBOW_FLEXION.description: Isolation movements can safely be taken closer to failure.

ELBOW_EXTENSION.description: Isolation work is well suited to low RIR training.

SHOULDER_ABDUCTION.description: Lateral raises respond well to training near failure.

CALF.description: Calves generally tolerate training very close to failure.

CORE_FLEXION.description: Maintain movement quality and spinal control.

ANTI_EXTENSION.description: Hold until the prescribed duration while maintaining proper posture.

CARRY.description: Progress by increasing load, distance, or duration rather than RIR.
